title: What is the correct syntax for include files?
body:
I have an include file (parameters.v) such as

```
`define DEPTH = 8
`define WIDTH = 198
```

I use these values in actual module such as

```
`include "parameters.v"

module foo(
input wire [`WIDTH-1:0] data,
input wire [`DEPTH-1:0] addr,
...
);

endmodule
```

When I run iverilog as

`iverilog -g2005 -I. sim/tb_foo.v foo.v`

it gives errors like

```
foo.v:12: syntax error
foo.v:1: Errors in port declarations.
foo.v:24: syntax error
foo.v:23: error: syntax error in parameter value assignment list.
foo.v:22: error: Invalid module instantiation
```
code_blocks:
`include "parameters.v"

module foo(
input wire [`WIDTH-1:0] data,
input wire [`DEPTH-1:0] addr,
...
);

endmodule

foo.v:12: syntax error
foo.v:1: Errors in port declarations.
foo.v:24: syntax error
foo.v:23: error: syntax error in parameter value assignment list.
foo.v:22: error: Invalid module instantiation
